figures.gloss: The singular God, Jesus Christ, in His glorified Divine Human form. Rejecting the orthodox Trinity of separate persons, Swedenborgianism posits God as a unified essence—Infinite Love and Infinite Wisdom—whose direct emanation sustains the universe like a spiritual sun.
figures.facet: Christ is not a sacrificial lamb satisfying divine wrath, but the visible form of the invisible God, making the Infinite comprehensible and approachable to human consciousness without splitting the divine unity.
figures.provenance: Emanuel Swedenborg's 'True Christian Religion' (1771); 'Divine Love and Wisdom' (1763).

figures.gloss: The fundamental law that the physical realm is a literal projection of the spiritual realm. Every natural object, historical event, and word in scripture perfectly correlates to a specific spiritual truth or state of divine affection.
figures.facet: It transforms the entire material universe and the literal text of the Bible into a divine cryptogram, asserting that nature is not independently real but merely a shadow cast by the spiritual mind.

figures.gloss: The collective form of Heaven. Swedenborg saw that the entire angelic heaven appears before the Lord as a single, gigantic human being. Every angelic society functions as a specific organ or tissue within this living celestial body.
figures.facet: Heaven is intensely anthropomorphic and organic; spiritual placement is determined by a soul's spiritual utility, effectively reducing the vastness of the afterlife into a single, perfectly integrated human anatomy.

figures.gloss: The intermediate realm where all souls awaken immediately after bodily death. Here, the external facades of worldly life are systematically stripped away until a person's inner character is fully revealed and aligned with their intellect.
figures.facet: It replaces purgatorial punishment with psychological unmasking. Souls are not judged by a tribunal but organically sort themselves based on their true desires before voluntarily descending to Hell or ascending to Heaven.

figures.gloss: The eternal destinations, which are not physical locations of reward or arbitrary torture, but the outward spatial projections of a soul's internal state. Heaven manifests love to the Lord and others; Hell manifests the love of self and the world.
figures.facet: God casts absolutely no one into hell. Damned spirits willingly throw themselves in, finding the divine atmosphere of Heaven agonizing, preferring the illusions, darkness, and madness of their own supreme egotism.

figures.gloss: The core, dominant affection that defines a human's fundamental identity. A person is not defined by their fleeting thoughts or outward pious actions, but by their deepest, innermost desire.
figures.facet: Salvation is strictly a matter of character transformation, not intellectual belief or vicarious atonement; in the afterlife, you inevitably become the exact psychological and spiritual geometry of what you love most.

figures.gloss: The eternal, spiritual marriage between a husband and wife, representing the perfect union of Wisdom (truth) and Love (goodness). True soulmates continue their union in Heaven, eventually appearing from a distance as a single radiant angel.
figures.facet: Unlike mainstream Christian theology where marriage dissolves at death, Swedenborg posits that gender and deeply intimate sexual union are eternal, cosmic principles necessary for full angelic existence and divine reflection.

figures.gloss: The 18th-century polymath and visionary who claimed his spiritual senses were opened by the Lord. For nearly thirty years, he consciously explored the afterlife, speaking with angels, demons, and spirits to document the true nature of reality.
figures.facet: He occupies a paradoxical role: a fiercely rational Enlightenment scientist who mapped the spiritual planes and angelic societies with the cool, empirical detachment of an anatomical textbook.

figures.gloss: Swedenborg's report, published as 'The Last Judgment' (1758), that the general judgment foretold in Revelation had already occurred — not on earth but in the intermediate World of Spirits — completed in the year 1757, closing an old spiritual order and clearing the way for a new church.
figures.facet: Reframes apocalypse as a completed event no living person witnessed except its one reporter: the New Church's entire self-understanding as a post-judgment dispensation rests on this single, unrepeated claim.
figures.provenance: Emanuel Swedenborg, 'The Last Judgment' (1758) and 'Continuation Concerning the Last Judgment' (1763).

figures.gloss: Revelation 21's holy city, read by Swedenborg not as any literal place but as a new dispensation of doctrine descending from heaven to earth — the New Church itself, inaugurated once the Last Judgment of 1757 cleared away the old spiritual order.
figures.facet: Supplies the tradition's own name for itself, the Church of the New Jerusalem: scripture's most famous end-times vision reinterpreted as this movement's founding charter rather than a future event.
figures.provenance: Emanuel Swedenborg, 'The New Jerusalem and Its Heavenly Doctrine' (1758).

figures.gloss: The doctrine that nothing — angel, spirit, or human — lives from itself: life flows continuously (influit) from the Lord through the spiritual world into the natural world and its minds, which only receive and modify what they are given, never originate it.
figures.facet: The mechanism running beneath every other doctrine in the system: correspondences hold, the Grand Man coheres, and ruling love organizes character only because influx is the continuous current underneath all three.

figures.gloss: The gradual, cooperative rebirth of a person's ruling love across a lifetime, worked jointly by the Lord and human free choice through repentance, instruction, and use — Swedenborg's replacement for both instantaneous evangelical conversion and vicarious atonement.
figures.facet: Makes salvation a lifelong process rather than a single moment or transaction: the roster's Ruling Love entry already names the destination this doctrine describes as the road there.
figures.provenance: Emanuel Swedenborg, 'True Christian Religion' (1771), chapters on Regeneration; 'Arcana Coelestia' (1749-1756).

figures.gloss: The teaching that love and wisdom become real only when performed as use — service rendered to others — so that angelic rank, human vocation, and even salvation are measured by uses done, not belief professed or status held.
figures.facet: Supplies the criterion the Grand Man entry invokes without naming: heaven is one integrated body precisely because every angel's placement is fixed by the specific use they perform.
figures.provenance: Emanuel Swedenborg, 'Divine Love and Wisdom' (1763), Part III on use; 'Heaven and Hell' (1758), sections on angelic employments.

figures.gloss: Frontier nurseryman (1774-1845) who planted apple-tree nurseries across Ohio, Indiana, and Pennsylvania while distributing New Church tracts and loose pages of Swedenborg's works to settlers along with his seedlings.
figures.facet: The tradition's one folk saint and a rare case of a small, doctrinally dense church owning a beloved national legend — most Americans who know the Appleseed name have never heard of the theology he was actually spreading.
figures.provenance: William Kerrigan, 'Johnny Appleseed and the American Orchard' (2012); New Church historical records documenting Chapman's membership and tract distribution.

edges.note: Intra-tradition bond: The Grand Man of Heaven is the direct reflection and macroscopic embodiment of the Divine Human's infinite form.
Intra-tradition bond: Heaven and Hell are nothing more than the spatialized, environmental projections of an individual's ruling love.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Swedenborg explicitly identifies his Divine Human with the historical Jesus Christ, reinterpreted not as a separate person of the Trinity but as the sole, integrated God.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Swedenborg directly equates Jehovah of the Old Testament with the inner soul of Jesus, asserting absolute identity rather than relational separation.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both conceptualize the supreme divine principle transitioning into a comprehensible human form (Nirmanakaya / Divine Human) without dividing its unified cosmic essence.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The identical Hermetic and Swedenborgian principle that every material phenomenon is a direct reflection of an invisible, higher spiritual reality.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A binary reality model where the visible, material world is viewed as a dependent shadow of underlying, invisible spiritual forces.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The cosmic man concept—the Vedic Purusha and the Swedenborgian Grand Man both envision the totality of existence as a single immense divine body.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both represent the divine fullness or totality of the spiritual realm, structured as an intricately organized system of emanations or angelic societies.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Dogon cosmology and Swedenborgianism both envision the fundamental structure of the universe as taking the form of an idealized, colossal human body.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Unlike Manichaean dualism where darkness is a co-eternal, rival substance, Swedenborg's Hell is entirely self-generated by the conscious withdrawal from Divine Love.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both systems feature heavily structured, multi-tiered cosmologies where a soul's placement is perfectly, mechanically dictated by its internal spiritual weight.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: While Buddhism dissolves the core self into non-self, Swedenborgianism crystallizes it, asserting that a singular, eternal 'ruling love' permanently defines personal identity.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both traditions locate the true essence of the individual not in outward bodily form but in an irreducible, eternal inner substance that shapes its own spiritual destiny.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The spiritual necessity of cosmic complementarity, where Love (feminine) and Wisdom (masculine) must inevitably unite to form a complete reality.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both involve a visionary practitioner deliberately and consciously traversing the unseen realms to acquire knowledge essential for the living.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Visionary founders who synthesized complex, systematic cosmologies claiming to be the final, pure revelation correcting previous theological distortions.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Visionary scribes who map out the hidden architecture of the universe, combining deep esotericism with a highly structured, nearly scientific cosmological framework.

Two 19th-century American-adjacent revelations replace a binary heaven/hell with graded afterlife states admitting nearly everyone to some degree of glory — Smith's three kingdoms and Swedenborg's heaven-and-hell-as-states, independently arrived at.
Davis's trance cosmology of ascending spheres explicitly builds on Swedenborg's visionary afterlife geography - the New Church's mysticism feeding directly into Spiritualism's own.
Intra-tradition bond: the completed judgment clears the way for the new dispensation named after it.
Intra-tradition bond: influx is the physics: correspondences describe what the continuous downward flow of life makes visible in matter and scripture alike.
Intra-tradition bond: regeneration is the lifelong remaking process; ruling love is the character it remakes.
Intra-tradition bond: the Grand Man's anatomy of angelic societies is organized entirely by the uses each performs.
Intra-tradition bond: the frontier disciple distributing the founder's own texts alongside his orchards.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: two traditions independently reinterpret a biblical end-times prophecy as an invisible event already completed in a specific year, unwitnessed by the living world.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: reality sustained by a continuous downward flow of divine life through graded vessels, structural convergence rather than shared descent.

Both a single prophet-founder whose visionary reception becomes the entire doctrinal and institutional charter of the tradition that follows him — structural parallel of the solitary-revelator pattern, no lineage claimed.

dossier.essence: The Christian tradition based on the theological writings of the Swedish scientist and visionary Emanuel Swedenborg (1688–1772), who from 1745 claimed his spiritual senses were opened to see heaven, hell, and the spiritual world directly. Organized after his death as the 'New Church' (the Church of the New Jerusalem, from the late 1780s in England), it is a small body teaching a non-Trinitarian Christianity in which God is one divine person, Jesus Christ in his glorified Divine Human.
dossier.cosmos: God is one — infinite Divine Love and Divine Wisdom — radiating like a spiritual sun; the natural world is a system of 'correspondences,' an outward projection of spiritual realities, so that scripture and nature alike carry an inner meaning. At death everyone passes through an intermediate 'World of Spirits' where the outer self is stripped away to reveal the innermost 'ruling love,' and souls then freely enter heaven or hell — no one is cast down by God — according to what they most love. Heaven appears before the Lord as one vast human form, the Maximus Homo, and true marriage love may continue eternally.
dossier.practice: The New Church is a worshipping Christian body, with baptism, the Holy Supper, preaching, and an ordained ministry, but its distinctive discipline is inward and doctrinal: reading Swedenborg's works as authoritative revelation, interpreting the Bible by its 'internal sense' through the doctrine of correspondences, and pursuing regeneration — the lifelong reshaping of one's ruling love toward love of God and neighbor, expressed as useful work (charity). Its cultural influence, from William Blake to the American frontier missionary John Chapman ('Johnny Appleseed'), has far outrun its formal membership.
dossier.canon: Swedenborg's Latin theological works — above all 'Arcana Coelestia' (1749–56), 'Heaven and Hell' (1758), 'Divine Love and Wisdom' (1763), 'Conjugial Love' (1768), and 'True Christian Religion' (1771) — read together with the Bible.
dossier.reflects: Revelation as system and cipher — the visible world read as a code of the spiritual, and heaven and hell relocated from external verdict to the soul's own ruling love.
sources: Ernst Benz, 'Emanuel Swedenborg: Visionary Savant in the Age of Reason' (2002)
Marguerite Beck Block, 'The New Church in the New World' (1932)
